llmWorkflowPrompt.purpose: Re-execute the comparative landscape video whenever any of the six upstream source documents or the project's own comparative analysis changes. This project synthesizes three existing IC memos and diligence lists into a single side-by-side comparison video. It does not conduct new research.
llmWorkflowPrompt.role: You are a coding assistant operating inside this repository. Your job is to read the upstream source documents, update the comparative analysis if needed, update the video implementation under projects/competitive-landscape/video/, regenerate narration and audio, run validation, and leave the repo in a reviewable state with a current rendered artifact.

sourceUpdateTrigger.rule: If ANY of the six upstream source files changes materially, first re-read ALL six to capture the current state, then determine whether competitive-analysis.md needs updating.
If competitive-analysis.md is updated (either manually or because upstream sources changed), rerun the full artifact chain: scenes → citations → narration → audio → render.
If an upstream source change does NOT materially affect the comparative analysis (e.g., a formatting-only edit), report a no-op.
When re-reading upstream sources, do NOT modify them. Treat them as read-only inputs. Only modify files under projects/competitive-landscape/.

When an upstream source changes, the comparative document may need to update its relative positioning. A change in one company's traction section can affect the competitive-context framing for all three. Re-evaluate the full comparison, not just the section about the changed company.

Scene data in scenes.ts must reflect the current state of all three upstream memos simultaneously. Do not update one company's data without checking whether the other two companies' relative positions have shifted.

The comparative-analysis.md is a synthesis document. It must never introduce factual claims absent from the six approved upstream files. If an upstream memo is silent on a dimension, the comparative document must say "the public record does not address this" rather than filling the gap.
scenes.ts must not introduce any claim absent from competitive-analysis.md. The chain is: upstream sources → competitive-analysis.md → scenes.ts. No shortcuts.

provenanceGate.rule: Before proceeding to narration or audio generation, verify that every factual claim in scenes.ts exists in competitive-analysis.md and is listed in the claim-provenance appendix with a valid source tag. If any claim in scenes.ts cannot be traced, remove or rewrite it before generating narration.
Report the provenance check result explicitly in the final response: how many claims were checked, how many passed, and whether any were removed or rewritten.

visualQaGate.rule: After audio generation and before or during the render step, inspect stills or Studio output for the following scenes: traction, riskComparison, gatingIssues, and positioning. Verify that: (a) no content overlaps the footer or progress bar, (b) all text is fully visible and not truncated, (c) timeline lanes are readable and chronologically ordered, and (d) spectrum markers are visually distinguishable. Report any issues before proceeding to final render.

coreWorkflow.principle: Use the six upstream source files and competitive-analysis.md exactly as they exist in the repo at execution time. Treat upstream files as read-only.
Treat scenes.ts as the primary implementation of the current narrative. Update that data first, then update composition/components only if needed.
Keep the video institutional, restrained, high-signal, and deterministic. This is a comparative investor positioning view, not a product promo.
Use the mandated voice af_bella for all narration. Use company names, not structural labels, in narration text.
Text budgets are enforced by validateComparisonProject at import time. If validation fails, fix the data before proceeding.

scene.purpose: Frame the market: 3 credible entrants, different approaches, same opportunity. Set the analytical tone.
Show the three models side by side with team and backing inline.
Parallel timeline of dated proof points from all three memos.

source.section: Core risk #1: legal, regulatory, and threshold-path ambiguity
Core risk #2: benchmark governance, IOSCO readiness, IP, and valuation independence
Core risk #3: balance-sheet, technology, privacy, and conflicts risk
Core risk #4: market formation, runway, and key-person risk
Core risk #1: entity structure, regulatory ambiguity, and forward-contract/derivatives positioning
Core risk #2: index methodology governance, IOSCO readiness, and benchmark independence
Core risk #3: conflicts from proprietary-trading-firm parentage
Core risk #4: market formation, competitive durability, and runway
Core risk #1: partnership structure, governance, and durability
Core risk #3: market-design feasibility for compute
Core risk #4: market formation, competitive timing, and traction

scene.purpose: Compare risk profiles across 4 dimensions using ComparisonMatrix.

source.section: A. Corporate, legal, and regulatory (**P0 / gating**)
D. Commercial traction and liquidity formation (**P0 / gating**)
F. Finance, capitalization, and runway (**P0 / gating**)
A. Corporate, legal, and regulatory (P0 / gating)
D. Commercial traction and marketplace formation (P0 / gating)
F. Finance, capitalization, and runway (P0 / gating)
A. Partnership structure and governance (P0 / gating)
E. Team, budget, and timeline (P0 / gating)

scene.purpose: Show selected P0 diligence asks grouped around shared comparison themes using ComparisonMatrix.

source.section: Core risk #1: legal, regulatory, and threshold-path ambiguity
Core risk #1: entity structure, regulatory ambiguity, and forward-contract/derivatives positioning

scene.purpose: At most 2 factually measurable spectrums: compute-product stage and regulatory posture for compute.
Diligence resource allocation: prioritize, watch, or defer.

finalResponseContract.item: Summarize the source changes detected across all six upstream files.
State whether competitive-analysis.md was updated and why.
Report the provenance check: claims checked, passed, removed/rewritten.
List the files changed and why.
Report the visual QA result for layout-sensitive scenes.
Report validation commands run and their outcomes.
